title: Post-072 Roadmap Trigger Realignment Decision
objective: Decide whether any immediate post-072 Olares governance slice is justified now that the host-workflow-hardening lane is explicitly dormant, or whether the remaining roadmap can be left as-is.
dependency_note: Packet 072 closed the host-workflow-hardening lane on explicit dormancy. The remaining question is whether the older roadmap now accurately reflects that trigger-based posture, or whether it still implies an always-open Olares backlog that should be corrected before a later session relies on it.
verdict_summary: A bounded roadmap-trigger realignment is justified. The current authority chain now treats the host-workflow-hardening lane as dormant until new evidence appears, but the older roadmap still presents several generic Phase 2 through Phase 4 items as blank open work. That mismatch is a real governance friction because it can misroute later Olares work selection.
rejected_as_next_active_lane: Reopening the dormant host-workflow-hardening lane without new operator friction.
Leaving the roadmap ambiguous even though the authority chain has already moved to trigger-based dormancy.
Opening runtime, install, hosting, or AI-boundary work by assumption.
next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 074 - Roadmap Trigger Realignment Execution
sql_permission_note: This decision packet authorizes no source/test runtime mutation, no package or lockfile mutation, no installs, no runtime or service mutation, no AI-services expansion, no remote rewrite, no rollback or force/reset/clean, and no old-clone mutation.
